---
# Read a nested JSON scalar, compute a ratio without infix math, and filter

**Dataset**: `guardian_xql_lab` (composite/array lookup lab)

`json_extract_scalar(meta_json, "$.geo.country")` reads a nested path (the dotted sugar `meta_json -> geo.country` is equivalent). The scalar comes back as a STRING even for JSON numbers, so wrap with `to_integer(...)` before comparing or `to_float(...)` before math. XQL has NO infix arithmetic — use `divide(a, b)` / `multiply(a, b)` (raw integer `divide` truncates, so cast with `to_float` for a real ratio). A JSON boolean reads back as the string `"false"`.

```sql
dataset = guardian_xql_lab
| alter country = json_extract_scalar(meta_json, "$.geo.country"), risk = to_integer(json_extract_scalar(meta_json, "$.risk")), mfa = json_extract_scalar(meta_json, "$.mfa")
| alter risk_ratio = divide(to_float(risk), 100.0)
| filter risk >= 80 and mfa = "false"
| fields username, country, risk, risk_ratio, mfa
| sort desc risk
```

_Live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant; measured cost ~0.0003 CU against a 10-row lookup (data scanned, not query complexity, drives CU)._
